member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.AttributeCredentialResolver
member.summary: Resolves provider credentials by scanning declarations on a provider type and reading the first matching non-empty environment variable from each attribute's list, with an optional config-value fallback.

remarks.para: This is the preferred credential resolution mechanism for new providers. The legacy per-provider credential resolver interface requires a dedicated method per provider and is preserved only for backward compatibility with existing providers.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.AttributeCredentialResolver.ForType(System.Type,System.Func{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Creates an for by reading all declarations on that type.
member.param: Optional fallback called with the logical credential name when no environment variable yields a value. Return if not available via config either.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.AttributeCredentialResolver.GetAttributes(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns all declarations on the given , including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.CredentialSchemaRegistry
member.summary: Bulk index of credential schemas declared via .

remarks.para: is the lightweight path for resolving credentials for a single provider type. is the companion discovery/index surface for scenarios that need to enumerate many provider credential schemas at once, such as UI catalogs.
Provider ID lookups are only available when a provider also declares . Providers without a data-source attribute remain available through the type-based index.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute
member.summary: Declares that a provider class requires a named credential at runtime. Apply one attribute per credential; providers with multiple credentials apply the attribute multiple times.

remarks.para: scans these attributes at startup to build a per-provider , eliminating the need to add a dedicated method to the legacy per-provider credential resolver for every new provider.
The WPF CredentialManagementPage reads these attributes to render credential fields dynamically; and are used as UI labels.
remarks.code: [RequiresCredential("API_KEY", EnvironmentVariables = new[] { "MYBROKER_API_KEY", "MYBROKER__APIKEY" }, DisplayName = "API Key", Description = "Found in your MyBroker dashboard under Settings → API.")] [RequiresCredential("SECRET_KEY", EnvironmentVariables = new[] { "MYBROKER_SECRET_KEY" }, Optional = true, DisplayName = "API Secret (optional)")] public sealed class MyBrokerHistoricalDataProvider : BaseHistoricalDataProvider { ... }

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute.Name
member.summary: Logical credential name used to look up the resolved value via .

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute.EnvironmentVariables
member.summary: One or more environment variable names to try in order (first non-empty value wins).

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute.Optional
member.summary: When the provider continues to function without this credential (e.g. anonymous / free-tier endpoints). Defaults to .

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute.DisplayName
member.summary: Human-readable label shown in the credential management UI. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute.Description
member.summary: Optional hint shown in the credential management UI, e.g. "Found under Settings → API Keys".

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Contracts.RequiresCredentialAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.param: Logical credential name, e.g. "API_KEY". Must be unique within the provider class.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.DataSources.DataSourceAttribute
member.summary: Marks a class as a data source provider for automatic discovery and registration. Classes decorated with this attribute will be automatically discovered and registered with the DI container when using AddDataSources().
member.remarks: This attribute is the foundation of ADR-005 (Attribute-Based Provider Discovery). It enables automatic provider registration and metadata capture at startup.

member.summary: Optional description of the data source.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderMetadata
member.summary: Unified metadata interface that all provider types implement. Enables consistent discovery, routing, and UI presentation across streaming, backfill, and symbol search providers.
member.remarks: This interface centralizes provider identity and capabilities into a single contract, eliminating special-case logic in the registry and UI layers. UI-specific properties (Notes, Warnings, CredentialFields) have default implementations returning empty arrays, allowing providers to optionally override them for richer UI presentation.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderMetadata.ProviderId
member.summary: Unique identifier for the provider (e.g., "alpaca", "polygon", "yahoo").

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderMetadata.ProviderDisplayName
member.summary: Human-readable display name.

member.summary: Description of the provider and its capabilities.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderMetadata.ProviderPriority
member.summary: Priority for routing and failover (lower = higher priority, tried first).

member.summary: Unified capability flags and metadata for this provider.

member.summary: Whether this provider requires credentials to operate.

member.summary: Credential fields required for this provider. Override to specify API keys, tokens, or other authentication requirements.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderModule
member.summary: Defines a self-describing provider module that bundles all capability types for a single external data provider (streaming, historical, symbol search, brokerage) behind one DI registration entry point.
member.remarks:
Implement once per external provider (e.g., AlpacaProviderModule). Use to discover and load modules from assemblies.
All new properties have default interface implementations so existing callers of remain source-compatible without changes.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderModule.ModuleId
member.summary: Unique provider identifier (e.g., "alpaca", "polygon"). Lower-case, no spaces. Default: derived from the implementing class name by stripping "ProviderModule".

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderModule.ModuleDisplayName
member.summary: Human-readable display name (e.g., "Alpaca Markets"). Default: derived from the implementing class name by stripping "ProviderModule".

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderModule.Capabilities
member.summary: Capabilities advertised by this module before registration. Used by for capability-based filtering and load diagnostics. An empty array means the module does not self-declare capabilities.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderModule.ValidateAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Validates that prerequisites for this module are satisfied before registration. Default implementation always returns .
member.remarks: Override to check credential availability, required services, or version compatibility. Returning an invalid result causes to skip this module and record a failure instead of throwing.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IProviderModule.Register(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Meridian.Infrastructure.DataSources.DataSourceRegistry)
member.summary: Register provider services into the DI container.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ModuleValidationResult
member.summary: Result of a module's self-validation step.
member.param: Whether the module passed validation and may proceed to registration.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ModuleValidationResult.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Result of a module's self-validation step.
member.param: Whether the module passed validation and may proceed to registration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ModuleValidationResult.IsValid
member.summary: Whether the module passed validation and may proceed to registration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ModuleValidationResult.FailureReason
member.summary: Human-readable explanation when is false.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ModuleValidationResult.Valid
member.summary: Singleton valid result — avoids repeated allocations on the happy path.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ModuleValidationResult.Failure(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a failure result with the specified reason.

member.summary: Discovers, validates, and loads implementations from one or more assemblies into a DI service collection.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.IMarketDataClient
member.summary: Market data client abstraction for provider-agnostic market data ingestion. Implementations must be non-blocking on publish paths.
member.remarks:
This interface is the core contract for ADR-001 (Provider Abstraction Pattern). All streaming data providers must implement this interface.
Implements for unified provider discovery and capability reporting across all provider types.

member.name: T:Meridian.ProviderSdk.IHistoricalBarWriter
member.summary: Abstraction for writing historical bar data to storage. Defined in ProviderSdk to break the circular dependency between Infrastructure and Storage projects. Storage implementations provide concrete writers; Infrastructure consumers inject this interface.
member.remarks: Resolves Phase 0.1: DataGapRepair.StoreBarsAsync was a no-op because Infrastructure could not reference Storage directly.

member.summary: Persists a batch of historical bars to the configured storage backend.
